sections.paragraph:
ἀμφότεροι, αι, α (DELG s.v. ἄμφω; Hom.+)

① both (Jos., Ant. 16, 125; Just.; Tat. 1, 2; Ath., R. 64, 30 al.) Dg 9:6. ἀ. συντηροῦνται both (i.e. wine and skins) are preserved Mt 9:17; Lk 5:38 v.l.; ἀ. εἰς βόθυνον (ἐμ-)πεσοῦνται both (the guide and the one whom he leads) will fall into the pit Mt 15:14; Lk 6:39. Cp. Mt 13:30.—ἦσαν δίκαιοι ἀ. Lk 1:6; cp. vs. 7 (the masc. form for a married couple, Ammonius, Vi. Aristot. p. 10, 6 Westerm.). ἀμφοτέροις ἐχαρίσατο 7:42; κατέβησαν ἀ. Ac 8:38; ἀ. οἱ νεανίσκοι GPt 9:37; ἀ. τὰ πλοῖα Lk 5:7; τὸν ἐπʼ ἀμφοτέροις θεόν God who is over both B 19:7; D 4:10.—οἱ ἀ. both together (Lat. utrimque; οἱ δύο ‘each … of the two’ [regarded severally], Lat. uterque, s. B-D-F §275, 8) ἀποκαταλλάσσειν τοὺς ἀ. τῷ θεῷ to reconcile both of them w. God Eph 2:16. οἱ ἀ. ἐν ἑνὶ πνεύματι vs. 18; τὰ ἀ. ἓν ποιεῖν vs. 14. Either D 7:3.

② all, even when more than two are involved (Diod S 1, 75, 1 πρὸς ἀμφότερα=for everything, in every respect; PThéad 26, 4 [296 a.d.]; PGen 67, 5; 69, 4) Ac 19:16. Φαρισαῖοι ὁμολογοῦσιν τὰ ἀ. believe in them all 23:8. JBury, ClR 11, 1897, 393ff; 15, 1901, 440, ByzZ 7, 1898, 469; 11, 1902, 111; Mlt. 80; Rdm. 77f.—M-M.

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

masc. masc. = masculine

Ammonius Ammonius , phil., V a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Lat. Lat. = Latin

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

PGen PGen = Les Papyrus de Genève—List 4

ClR ClR = Classical Review—List 6

ByzZ ByzZ = Byzantinische Zeitschrift—List 6

Mlt. Mlt. = JMoulton; with page number alone specified, this abbr. refers to A Grammar of NT Greek I: Prolegomena—List 6

Rdm. Rdm. = LRadermacher, Neutestamentliche Grammatik—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 55
